visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The scene opens with a close-up of a large flat-screen monitor displaying a high-definition video game. The game features futuristic, high-tech wheelchairs competing in a neon-lit basketball arena. In the foreground, a pair of hands wearing bracelets and a yellow smartwatch are visible, holding two detached game controllers and moving them with rapid, precise gestures. (2) The camera pulls back to reveal a man from a back-side perspective, wearing a grey flat cap and a matching grey hoodie. He is seated at a white table with a black mat, intensely focused on the screen while physically maneuvering the controllers in mid-air to command the on-screen action. The setting appears to be a brightly lit indoor exhibition hall with industrial-style ceiling structures in the background. (3) The perspective shifts between shots of the game screen and the player's physical movements. The game environment on the monitor shows multiple robotic characters in wheelchairs bumping into each other and shooting for a hoop. The player continues to flick his wrists and move his arms, demonstrating motion-controlled gameplay mechanics. (4) The video concludes with the player successfully making a shot in the game, followed by him pointing at the screen and briefly turning his head. The lighting remains consistent, high-key studio lighting throughout the entire sequence.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Slogan]: 任天堂Switch2輪椅籃球🏀實際玩

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: One main creator/player is visible throughout the video, primarily from the back or showing only hands.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: The main character is a man wearing a grey flat cap, a grey zip-up hoodie, a yellow smartwatch on his left wrist, and multiple wooden bead bracelets.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The background consists of a large, high-ceilinged indoor space resembling an exhibition hall or convention center, with visible metal scaffolding and large white partitions. The immediate area features a red wall where the monitor is mounted and a white table with a black gaming mat.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) High-tempo electronic music with a strong synth bassline begins, accompanied by high-arousal Japanese voices shouting excitedly. The sound of digital game UI elements clicking and a 'whoosh' effect is clearly audible over the music. (2) A sharp 'thud' sound effect from the game occurs, followed by an immediate increase in crowd cheering and laughter. The primary voices maintain a fast speech rate, frequently using rising intonations to express surprise and thrill. (3) The acoustic texture shifts slightly as the camera moves, picking up more of the ambient room tone of a large hall, yet the game SFX of wheelchair collisions (metallic clinks) and synthesized 'power-up' sounds remain prominent. (4) A final 'ding' bell-like sound effect signals a point scored in the game, triggering a louder wave of applause and high-pitched cheers from the surrounding crowd. The electronic BGM continues steadily at around 128 BPM until the end.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video aims to showcase the immersive and unique gameplay of a wheelchair basketball game on the Nintendo Switch, highlighting its motion control capabilities and the high-energy atmosphere of a live demo event.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The video features a prominent display of the Nintendo brand and focuses entirely on the gameplay experience of a specific title.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video effectively uses dual-perspective shots (screen vs. player) to demonstrate the physical interaction required for the game.
